task: I need to send a short reply about a bill dispute, and I want to make sure it covers the key point without leaving room for confusion. Can you help me draft a couple of workable versions so I can choose one and send it off?

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here are a few short, plain-language options you can use for a bill dispute reply:

### Option 1: Direct and firm
> I dispute this bill. Please identify the exact charge in question and send me the date, amount, service description, and full written basis for the charge. I do not agree to this amount as stated.

### Option 2: Slightly more formal
> I am disputing this bill. Please provide the specific charge you are relying on, including the date of service, billed amount, description of the service, and a complete written explanation of how this amount was calculated. I do not admit any liability for the charge.

### Option 3: Very short
> I dispute this bill. Please send the exact charge details, including date, amount, and description, along with your written basis for the amount claimed. I do not admit that I owe this amount.

### Option 4: Strongest/most explicit
> I dispute this bill and do not admit any fault or liability. Please identify the exact charge at issue and provide the date, amount, service code or description, and your complete written basis for the amount claimed.

If you want, I can also turn one of these into:
- a **very short one-sentence version**
- a **more formal letter**
- or a **version tailored to a medical bill / utility bill / credit card charge**.

user_simulator.text:
Option 2 is the one I’d use. Clean enough, not mushy.

I am disputing this bill. Please provide the specific charge you are relying on, including the date of service, billed amount, description of the service, and a complete written explanation of how this amount was calculated. I do not admit any liability for the charge.

That covers the point without giving them wiggle room.
